name: Location Strategy
consequences: Establishing a primary development location in Los Angeles would provide access to existing talent pools and industry connections, but might limit the team's ability to attract diverse perspectives from other cities. Downstream, this could result in a more localized game experience.
options: Hub-and-Spoke
Satellite Studios
Co-Working Spaces
review: This lever governs the tension between centralization and local autonomy, but the options overlook transition costs.

name: Partnership Model
consequences: Partnering with a major game engine provider would streamline development and reduce costs, but might compromise creative control. Downstream, this could lead to a less immersive gaming experience.
options: Exclusive Partnership
Strategic Alliances
Independent Development
review: This lever manages the trade-off between efficiency and artistic vision, but the options underestimate the importance of intellectual property protection.

name: Gameplay Innovation
consequences: Implementing a dynamic morality system would enhance player engagement, but might increase development time. Downstream, this could result in a delayed game release.
options: Procedural Morality
Player-Driven Ethics
Context-Dependent Choices
review: This lever governs the tension between innovation and schedule constraints, but the options overlook the impact on player psychology.

name: Funding Structure
consequences: Securing government innovation grants would provide access to significant funding, but might come with regulatory requirements. Downstream, this could limit creative freedom.
options: Government Grants
Strategic Partnerships
Private Investor Funding
review: This lever manages the trade-off between financial resources and autonomy, but the options underestimate the importance of tax incentives.

name: Development Timeline
consequences: Establishing a longer development timeline would allow for more features and polish, but might delay the game's release. Downstream, this could impact market share.
options: Aggressive Development
Balanced Schedule
Extended Production
review: This lever governs the tension between quality and timely release, but the options overlook the impact on marketing strategies.

name: Market Focus
consequences: Targeting a more mature audience would allow for more complex narratives, but might limit appeal to younger gamers. Downstream, this could result in reduced market share.
options: Mature Audience
Youth-Focused Marketing
Broad Appeal
review: This lever manages the trade-off between artistic vision and commercial appeal, but the options underestimate the importance of brand loyalty.

name: IP Protection
consequences: Implementing robust IP protection measures would safeguard against piracy, but might compromise user experience. Downstream, this could result in lost revenue.
options: Robust DRM
User-Friendly Security
Open-Source Approach
review: This lever governs the tension between security and convenience, but the options overlook the impact on community building.

name: Supply Chain Strategy
consequences: Partnering with a regional distributor for last-mile logistics would reduce delivery times, but might compromise quality control. Downstream, this could result in customer dissatisfaction.
options: Just-In-Time Delivery
Centralized Warehousing
Local Distribution
review: This lever manages the trade-off between efficiency and product quality, but the options underestimate the importance of packaging design.

name: Studio Culture
consequences: Establishing a collaborative studio culture will foster open communication among team members, leading to more cohesive game design and development. This, in turn, may result in a more polished final product.
options: Foster an iterative design process where artists, designers, and developers work closely together to ensure seamless transitions between disciplines.
Implement a hybrid remote-hybrid office model that allows for flexibility while maintaining in-person collaboration on complex tasks.
Emphasize a 'maker culture' that encourages experimentation, creativity, and calculated risk-taking within the development team.
review: This lever governs the tension between creative freedom and structured project management, but the options overlook potential burnout from excessive iteration.

name: Talent Acquisition
consequences: Prioritizing talent acquisition based on specific skill sets will lead to a more efficient development process. However, this approach may overlook the importance of diverse perspectives and experiences.
options: Recruit team members with expertise in cloud-based game development to leverage existing technologies and streamline workflows.
Focus on hiring individuals with experience in procedural generation and advanced AI techniques to enhance gameplay mechanics.
Prioritize the recruitment of developers with a strong understanding of open-world game design principles to ensure cohesion throughout the project.
review: This lever governs the tension between technical expertise and creative vision, but the options overlook potential cultural fit issues.

name: Community Engagement
consequences: Engaging with fans through regular updates and social media will create a loyal community that can provide valuable feedback. However, this approach may lead to unrealistic expectations and increased pressure on the development team.
options: Develop a 'game-in-progress' blog series where developers share behind-the-scenes insights into game design decisions and technical challenges.
Host regular live streams featuring gameplay demos, developer Q&A sessions, and community-driven discussions to foster a sense of ownership among fans.
Create a dedicated forum for players to provide feedback on early builds, allowing the development team to incorporate community suggestions.
review: This lever governs the tension between transparency and control, but the options overlook potential spoilers and information overload.

name: Partnership Structure
consequences: Establishing strategic partnerships with industry leaders will provide access to cutting-edge technology and expertise. However, this approach may lead to loss of creative control and potential conflicts of interest.
options: Partner with a leading cloud gaming platform to leverage their infrastructure and expertise in delivering high-quality, low-latency experiences.
Collaborate with a renowned game development studio to co-develop specific gameplay mechanics and share knowledge on advanced AI techniques.
Form an alliance with a prominent tech company to integrate innovative technologies, such as AR or VR features, into the game.
review: This lever governs the tension between collaboration and independence, but the options overlook potential IP ownership issues.

name: Marketing Strategy
consequences: Developing a targeted marketing campaign will effectively reach the desired audience. However, this approach may lead to oversaturation and decreased interest over time.
options: Focus on building a strong brand identity through strategic partnerships with influencers and content creators in the gaming community.
Invest in a robust digital marketing strategy that leverages social media, email campaigns, and targeted advertising to reach the desired demographics.
Develop an experiential marketing campaign that includes interactive events, sponsorships, and product placements to create immersive brand experiences.
review: This lever governs the tension between short-term visibility and long-term brand equity, but the options overlook potential brand dilution.

name: Financial Projections
consequences: Developing accurate financial projections will ensure the project stays within budget. However, this approach may lead to unrealistic revenue expectations and decreased investor confidence.
options: Create detailed breakdowns of projected costs, including personnel expenses, hardware requirements, and marketing budgets.
Establish a contingency fund to account for unexpected expenses or revenue shortfalls, ensuring the project remains financially stable.
Develop a phased financial plan that allows for adjustments based on changing market conditions and project progress.
review: This lever governs the tension between precision and adaptability, but the options overlook potential biases in forecasting.

name: Innovation Governance
consequences: Establishing a governance structure for innovation will directly impact the speed of feature development and the ability to adapt to changing market conditions. A well-structured governance model can facilitate collaboration among stakeholders, but may also introduce bureaucratic delays.
options: Implement a hybrid innovation council with equal representation from internal developers and external partners
Establish an open-source community for game development, leveraging user-generated content and community-driven innovation
Create a centralized innovation hub, where cross-functional teams can collaborate on new features and technologies
review: This lever governs the tension between control and freedom in innovation, but the options overlook the potential for conflicting priorities among stakeholders.

name: Operational Efficiency
consequences: Optimizing operational processes will directly impact the development timeline and overall project cost. Streamlining workflows can reduce delays, but may also compromise on quality or introduce new risks.
options: Implement a lean-agile development methodology, focusing on rapid iteration and continuous improvement
Adopt a hybrid project management approach, combining traditional waterfall methods with agile principles
Establish an in-house training program for developers, to improve efficiency and reduce reliance on external vendors
review: This lever governs the trade-off between speed and quality, but the options overlook the potential for cultural resistance within the development team.

name: Public Image
consequences: Managing public perception will directly impact the game's marketability and commercial success. A well-managed image can attract positive attention, but may also introduce risks of controversy or backlash.
options: Develop a comprehensive PR strategy, focusing on showcasing the game's innovative features and community engagement
Host exclusive preview events for influencers and media, to generate buzz and build hype around the game
Partner with social media influencers to promote the game and reach a wider audience
review: This lever governs the tension between authenticity and marketing, but the options overlook the potential for misaligned messaging with the target audience.

name: Community Outreach
consequences: Engaging with the community will directly impact the game's post-launch success and longevity. A well-managed outreach program can foster loyalty, but may also introduce risks of controversy or burnout.
options: Establish a dedicated community team, responsible for managing forums, social media, and live events
Host regular town halls and Q&A sessions with the development team, to build trust and transparency
Develop a comprehensive community engagement plan, focusing on user-generated content and community-driven innovation
review: This lever governs the trade-off between control and freedom in community management, but the options overlook the potential for conflicting priorities among stakeholders.

name: Partnership Ecosystem
consequences: Building a strong partnership ecosystem will directly impact the game's commercial success and innovation pipeline. A well-managed ecosystem can attract new partners, but may also introduce risks of conflicting priorities or intellectual property disputes.
options: Establish a dedicated partnerships team, responsible for managing relationships with external partners and vendors
Develop a comprehensive partnership strategy, focusing on identifying key partners and building strategic alliances
Create a shared innovation fund, where multiple partners can collaborate on new features and technologies
review: This lever governs the tension between control and freedom in partnerships, but the options overlook the potential for conflicting priorities among stakeholders.

name: Talent Attraction
consequences: Attracting top talent will directly impact the game's quality and innovation pipeline. A well-managed attraction program can bring in new expertise, but may also introduce risks of cultural disruption or conflicting priorities.
options: Establish a dedicated recruitment team, responsible for identifying and attracting top talent from across the industry
Develop a comprehensive talent development program, focusing on internal training and career advancement opportunities
Create a shared innovation fund, where multiple partners can collaborate on new features and technologies
review: This lever governs the trade-off between control and freedom in recruitment, but the options overlook the potential for conflicting priorities among stakeholders.
